note: Structural baselines are backend-agnostic (occupancy classification + connected components are invariant under sub-pixel CPU<->GPU jitter), so these thresholds apply to linux-debug too. The PNGs under macos-debug/ are the Metal capture used to derive them. ROIs are framebuffer pixels (2560x1440), top-left origin.

shadow._metric: scripts/render-shadow-metric.py (#1765): magenta=shadowed, black=lit. hole_ratio = lit/(lit+shadow) inside the ROI; components = 4-connected shadow blobs; largest_frac = biggest blob / shadow_px. Clean cast shadow ~= few components, largest_frac near 1.
shadow.verdict: REPRODUCES — the cast shadow on the SDF floor shatters into 62 4-connected components (largest only 52% of shadow pixels) in a tight ROI; a clean cast shadow is a handful of large blobs. Swiss-cheese / dithered cast shadow (#1717 items 3-4) persists post-#1734.

coverage._metric: interior see-through holes inside a re-voxelized solid silhouette: classify pixels as background (near-black / near-floor-gray) vs solid; flood-fill background from the ROI border (exterior); enclosed background = interior holes. fill_ratio = solid / (solid + interior_holes). Clean solid ~= fill_ratio near 1, interior_hole_ratio near 0. The reusable, unit-tested coverage metric is T-3 (#1769); this is the T-1 baseline measurement.
coverage.verdict: DOES NOT REPRODUCE — the dense grounded re-voxelize cube is 100% covered (0 interior holes); the carved/multi-color L-prism is 99.1% covered (0.9% interior holes = the documented fine round-to-cell speckle, sub-cell CPU<->GPU divergence, expected per P1/P3). No #1619-class missing-face holes on the detached/thin re-voxelize path. The #1619 GRID-half fix (#1732) holds.

magenta_anomaly: EXPLAINED — the lone magenta/purple entity in a normal-colour capture is the #1596 (P4b-3) grounded world-placed cast-proof re-voxelize cube, intentionally tinted Color{210,120,255} so its cast shadow is identifiable beside the GRID cubes'. It is NOT the SHADOW debug overlay magenta (vec3(1,0,1) = 255,0,255), which only appears under --debug-overlay shadow. The issue premise ('only in-tree magenta source is the SHADOW debug overlay') is incomplete: this entity tint is a second source.
